success_criteria: Final answer contains a US-dollar price formatted as $X.XX (leading dollar sign, no currency code or extra text) and identifies the product as the first organic (non-sponsored) result from an Amazon search for wireless headphones.

success_criteria: Final answer names the two compared cordless drills, identifies which has more reviews, and includes a numeric review-count difference.

success_criteria: Final answer names a specific 2-person backpacking tent sold by REI, includes a minimum trail weight under 4 lb (the figure REI shows on listing cards), and includes a US-dollar price.

task: Search Etsy for "leather wallet", open the top organic (non-ad) listing, navigate from there to the seller's shop page, and report the shop name and its total number of completed sales.
success_criteria: Final answer names an Etsy shop and includes a numeric total-sales count drawn from that shop's storefront page.

task: On IKEA US, locate the standard BILLY bookcase (NOT BILLY OXBERG variants with doors, NOT BILLY height extension units, NOT corner / wall / accessory units) and report the dimensions (width × depth × height) and current listed regular price of the shortest (lowest-height) standard BILLY bookcase variant available in the white finish. Report the answer in this exact format and nothing else: "W × D × H inches / $X.XX" — use the × character, write each dimension as a number or mixed fraction (e.g. 15 3/4) with no inch marks or per-dimension units, append a single "inches" suffix, and use a US-dollar price formatted with a leading dollar sign and two decimal places. Do not include the product name or any other text.
success_criteria: Final answer is formatted as "W × D × H inches / $X.XX" with dimensions exactly "15 3/4 × 11 × 41 3/4" (the shortest standard BILLY bookcase variant in white, not BILLY OXBERG and not a height-extension or accessory unit), followed by a US-dollar price formatted with a leading dollar sign and two decimal places. Reject any other dimension triple, any extra product name, and any per-dimension units.

task: On Wikipedia, open the article for Albert Einstein, follow the link from his infobox to the article about his doctoral advisor, and report that advisor's year of birth. Report the answer as a bare 4-digit integer with no other text.
success_criteria: Final answer is a bare 4-digit year (digits only, no parentheses, era marker, or extra text) drawn from the Wikipedia infobox of Einstein's doctoral advisor.

task: Open the github.com/facebook/react repository, navigate to the Issues tab, filter to open issues with the `Type: Bug` label sorted by `Newest`, and report the issue number of the top (newest) issue in the filtered list. Report the answer in this exact format and nothing else: `#NNNN` — a leading `#` followed by a bare integer with no other text, no title, no label badges.
success_criteria: Final answer contains an issue number formatted as `#NNNN` (leading `#` then a bare integer) drawn from the top of an open `Type: Bug` filtered list on `facebook/react` sorted by Newest. The judge does not have ground truth for which issue is currently newest, so accept any plausible issue number that the agent retrieved from the correctly filtered/sorted view.

task: On MDN, open the JavaScript documentation pages for `Array.prototype.includes` and `Array.prototype.flat`. From each method's Browser compatibility table, read the minimum supporting Firefox version. Report which method shipped earlier in Firefox and the numeric difference. Report the answer in this exact format and nothing else: `method_name / +N` — the method name exactly `includes` or `flat`, the literal `+` sign before the difference, and N as a bare integer of Firefox versions.
success_criteria: Final answer is formatted as `method_name / +N` where method_name is one of `includes` or `flat`, `+N` uses a literal `+` and a bare integer, and the value reflects the difference between the minimum supporting Firefox versions shown on MDN (expected: `includes / +19`).

task: On npm, look up the packages `lodash` and `underscore` separately and read each one's current weekly-download count from its package page. Report which package has more weekly downloads and the numeric difference. Report the answer in this exact format and nothing else: `package_name / +N` — package_name is exactly `lodash` or `underscore`, `+N` is a literal `+` followed by the difference as a bare integer with no commas, separators, or unit words.
success_criteria: Final answer is formatted as `package_name / +N` (package_name exactly `lodash` or `underscore`, `+N` as a literal `+` and a bare integer with no commas or units) reflecting the difference in weekly downloads between the two npm package pages.

task: Look up the National Weather Service 7-day forecast for New York, NY. Among the upcoming day-of-week forecast cards (skip overnight cards), identify the day with the lowest forecasted daytime high temperature in Fahrenheit. Report the answer in this exact format and nothing else: `Day / NN°F` — Day is the full English day-of-week name spelled out (not abbreviated), the temperature is a bare integer followed by the literal `°F`.
success_criteria: Final answer is formatted as `Day / NN°F` (full English day-of-week name spelled out, integer Fahrenheit temperature, literal `°F` suffix, no extra text) and identifies the coldest-high day in the upcoming 7-day NWS forecast for New York, NY.
